questions.q: Director Mani Ratnam is most closely associated with which language industry of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam is best known as a leading writer-director of Tamil cinema, though he has also made Hindi films.

questions.q: Which composer made his feature film debut with the score for Mani Ratnam's Roja (1992)?
questions.explanation: Roja marked the feature film debut of composer A. R. Rahman, who went on to win two Academy Awards for Slumdog Millionaire.

questions.q: In which year was Mani Ratnam's acclaimed gangster film Nayakan released?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam's gangster drama Nayakan was released in 1987.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role of the Bombay don in Mani Ratnam's Nayakan (1987)?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played Velu Naicker, the don loosely inspired by Varadarajan Mudaliar, in Nayakan.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's films Roja, Bombay and Dil Se are often grouped together under which label?
questions.explanation: Roja (1992), Bombay (1995) and Dil Se (1998) are commonly described as Mani Ratnam's terrorism trilogy.

questions.q: In which year was Mani Ratnam born?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam was born in 1956.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 1998 film Dil Se was made primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: Dil Se (1998) was a Hindi-language film, part of Mani Ratnam's terrorism trilogy.

questions.q: Which 1995 Mani Ratnam film dealt with a Hindu-Muslim couple set against the backdrop of communal riots?
questions.explanation: Bombay (1995) portrayed an interfaith couple amid the communal violence in the city.

questions.q: What is the profession most associated with Mani Ratnam in the film industry?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam is a writer-director, known for both scripting and directing his films.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's films are noted for combining refined visual craft with which kind of subject matter?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam is noted for pairing political and personal themes with polished visual craft.

questions.q: Which actress played the title role of the village bride in Mani Ratnam's Roja (1992)?
questions.explanation: Madhoo played Roja, the young wife whose husband is kidnapped by militants in Kashmir.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 1992 film Roja centred on militancy in which Indian region?
questions.explanation: Roja followed a woman trying to free her husband after he is abducted by militants in Kashmir.

questions.q: Which 2000 Mani Ratnam Tamil romance was later remade in Hindi as Saathiya (2002)?
questions.explanation: Alaipayuthey (2000), starring Madhavan and Shalini, was remade in Hindi as Saathiya (2002).

questions.q: Which actor headlined Mani Ratnam's 1991 film Thalapathi alongside Mammootty?
questions.explanation: Thalapathi (1991) starred Rajinikanth as the protagonist, with Mammootty as the politician Devaraj.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 1997 film Iruvar drew inspiration from the relationship between a film star and a political leader in which state's politics?
questions.explanation: Iruvar (1997) was inspired by the lives of M. G. Ramachandran and M. Karunanidhi in Tamil Nadu politics.

questions.q: Which actress made her film acting debut in Mani Ratnam's Iruvar (1997)?
questions.explanation: Iruvar marked the film acting debut of Aishwarya Rai, who appeared in a dual role opposite Mohanlal.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam directed which 2010 bilingual film loosely based on the Ramayana, shot in Tamil and Hindi?
questions.explanation: Raavan (Hindi) and Raavanan (Tamil) were 2010 Mani Ratnam films reimagining the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which 2007 Mani Ratnam Hindi film, loosely inspired by the rise of a business tycoon, starred Abhishek Bachchan and Aishwarya Rai?
questions.explanation: Guru (2007) followed an ambitious entrepreneur, widely seen as inspired by Dhirubhai Ambani.

questions.q: Which production company did Mani Ratnam establish to produce his films?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam founded the production house Madras Talkies.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam is married to which actress, who appeared in films such as Mouna Ragam and Agni Natchathiram?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam is married to actress Suhasini, who is known professionally as Suhasini Maniratnam.

questions.q: Which song from Mani Ratnam's Dil Se, picturised on a moving train, became internationally famous?
questions.explanation: Chaiyya Chaiyya, composed by A. R. Rahman, was filmed atop a moving train in Dil Se (1998).

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's two-part historical epic based on Kalki Krishnamurthy's classic Tamil novel, the first part of which released in 2022, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Ponniyin Selvan, based on Kalki Krishnamurthy's novel, was released by Mani Ratnam in two parts (2022 and 2023).

questions.q: Before becoming a filmmaker, Mani Ratnam earned a postgraduate degree in which field?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam holds an MBA and worked briefly as a management consultant before entering films.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's directorial debut was which 1983 Kannada-language film?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam made his directorial debut with the Kannada film Pallavi Anu Pallavi in 1983.

questions.q: Which 1986 Mani Ratnam Tamil film, starring Revathi, follows a newlywed woman struggling to accept her arranged marriage while mourning a lost love?
questions.explanation: Mouna Ragam (1986), starring Revathi and Mohan, follows a young bride grieving a dead lover as she resists her arranged marriage.

questions.q: Which cinematographer shot several of Mani Ratnam's 1980s films, including Nayakan (1987) and Agni Natchathiram (1988)?
questions.explanation: P. C. Sreeram shot several of Mani Ratnam's 1980s films, including Nayakan and the lighting-driven Agni Natchathiram.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 1990 film Anjali centred on a family raising a child with what circumstance?
questions.explanation: Anjali (1990) told the story of a family caring for a terminally ill, developmentally challenged child.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 1997 Tamil film Iruvar and several later works were shot by which cinematographer, who later directed his own films?
questions.explanation: Santosh Sivan was the cinematographer on Iruvar and other Mani Ratnam films, and is himself a noted director.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam was awarded the Padma Shri, an Indian civilian honour, in which year?
questions.explanation: Mani Ratnam received the Padma Shri in 2002 for his contributions to Indian cinema.

questions.q: Mani Ratnam's 2004 Hindi film Yuva was simultaneously made in Tamil under which title?
questions.explanation: Yuva (2004) was shot simultaneously in Tamil as Aaytha Ezhuthu, with a different cast.
